input_candidates.truth_value: Table 1 summarizes literature on corrosion of LDSs with various alloying additions (Cr, Mo, Ni, Cu) and their effects on passive film and pitting resistance.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The fine-grained sample S950 forms an Al-rich oxide film with thickness 25.2 ± 1.2 nm and the lowest defect densities (acceptor density N_A 0.68×10²¹/cm³ and donor density N_D 0.26×10²¹/cm³), while the coarse-grained S1150 film thickness is 18 ± 0.9 nm with reduced Al content (from 90.17 at% to 69.91 at%) and higher defect density.

input_candidates.truth_value: Figure 4 grain size distribution data show heterogeneous distribution for S0 and uniform distributions for S950–S1150; exact average grain size of S1050 not numerically given but lies between 8.7 and 32 μm.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Potentiodynamic polarization breakdown current densities (i_bre) in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution: S0 9.92 μA/cm², S950 2.72 μA/cm², S1050 6.55 μA/cm², S1150 8.84 μA/cm².

input_candidates.truth_value: KAM value decreases from 1.62 (S0) to ~0.4 after solution treatment (Fig. 4e).
input_candidates.prediction_value: EIS polarization resistance (R_p) of as-polished samples: S0 6036 Ω·cm², S950 12486 Ω·cm², S1050 8014 Ω·cm², S1150 6992 Ω·cm²; after 7200 s potentiostatic polarization: S0 6143, S950 21355, S1050 14144, S1150 10756 Ω·cm².

input_candidates.truth_value: EIS equivalent circuit fitting parameters (n1, n2, Q1, Q2) are given in Tables 2 and 3 for all samples.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Oxide film protection efficiency (PE) calculated from R2 values after potentiostatic polarization: S950 30.75 %, S1150 17.95 %.

input_candidates.truth_value: Cyclic voltammetry shows Al oxidation peak at −0.81 V_SCE and Fe oxidation peak at −0.56 V_SCE; integrated charge density for Al peak in first cycle indicates more complete Al oxidation for S950.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Mott-Schottky analysis shows duplex semiconductor behavior (n-type in the potential region -1.13 to -0.93 V_SCE and p-type in -0.93 to -0.67 V_SCE) for all samples; carrier densities increase with grain size: S950 N_D 0.26×10²¹/cm³ and N_A 0.68×10²¹/cm³; S1150 N_D 0.49×10²¹/cm³ and N_A 1.25×10²¹/cm³.

input_candidates.truth_value: The Mott-Schottky analysis carrier densities ND and NA are shown in Fig. 12b.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Cyclic voltammetry reveals an Al oxidation peak at approximately -0.81 V_SCE and an Fe oxidation peak at approximately -0.56 V_SCE; S950 exhibits the highest Al oxidation charge density in the first cycle (2.55×10⁻⁵ C/cm²), which suppresses the Fe oxidation peak in the first cycle.

input_candidates.truth_value: The protective efficiency (PE) calculated from oxide film resistance is 30.75% for S950 and 17.95% for S1150.
input_candidates.prediction_value: During immersion testing (0.5–168 h) in 3.5 wt% NaCl, EIS R_p of S950 reaches 114.9 kΩ·cm² at 168 h, significantly higher than S1150 (50.6 kΩ·cm²), indicating rapid formation of a dense oxide film on fine-grained steel.

input_candidates.truth_value: Grain growth model Eq. (8)-(9) used to describe grain size evolution with temperature.
input_candidates.prediction_value: XPS valence state analysis of oxide films: Fe exists as Fe²⁺ (709.8 eV), Fe³⁺ (711.5 eV), and Fe_hy³⁺ (713.3 eV); Mn as Mn²⁺ (640.4 eV), Mn⁴⁺ (641.8 eV), and satellite peak (646.2 eV) except in S950 where only Mn²⁺ is detected; Al as Al³⁺ (74.1 eV); O as OH⁻ (531.6 eV), H₂O (533.2 eV), and O²⁻ (529.7 eV).

input_candidates.truth_value: Oxide film formation mechanism: preferential dissolution of Al at grain boundaries, high GB density promotes uniform and rapid film growth.
input_candidates.prediction_value: SKPFM surface potential measurements show HAGBs have the lowest potential (most active anodic dissolution sites), while ATBs exhibit a higher potential than the matrix with an approximately 30 mV potential difference that drives microgalvanic coupling and localized dissolution along twin boundaries.

input_candidates.truth_value: Figure 20 schematics illustrate the difference in oxide film formation and corrosion mechanisms between fine- and coarse-grained structures.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The corrosion mechanism schematic indicates that fine-grained structures (S950) with high GB density facilitate preferential Al dissolution at grain boundaries, yielding a continuous, dense Al-rich oxide film, whereas coarse-grained structures (S1150) with sparse boundaries develop thinner, discontinuous oxide films with higher Fe/Mn content and defect channels.
